summary: Crosscheck review reconciliation now has actual distribution ZIP provenance, full-suite evidence regeneration, ZIP-member batch freshness verification, recursive tmp workspace hygiene, archived run telemetry discovery, full-suite release-batch evidence separation, a primary-target touch promotion guard, and a schema-backed learning delta scoreboard.

observations.problem: The manifest can compute current distribution ZIP SHA-256 and entry count from a ZIP path; this closeout supplied and regenerated the actual distribution ZIP.
observations.why_it_matters: Without a final ZIP path supplied at package time, current_distribution_zip can remain stale even when code-level provenance sealing is implemented.
observations.automation_candidate: Wire EXTERNAL_REPORT_CURRENT_DISTRIBUTION_ZIP_PATH into the final packaging/review archive handoff so the manifest is regenerated immediately after the ZIP is created.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep external-report-reference-manifest wired to EXTERNAL_REPORT_CURRENT_DISTRIBUTION_ZIP_PATH during final release packaging, and verify current_distribution_zip matches the packaged ZIP SHA-256 and entry count.

observations.problem: The review report's full-suite evidence gap was closed by regenerating the canonical full-suite summary and keeping it separate from report-contract summary evidence.
observations.why_it_matters: The checked-in targeted report-contract summary must not be interpreted as full release-builder evidence.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a shard-first full-suite evidence regeneration path that writes shard JSON, supports interrupted resume, and promotes a represents_full_suite=true aggregate only after all shards finish.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep ops/reports/test-execution-summary-full.json as a separate release-batch input; rerun the full-suite or shard aggregate regeneration before release signoff.

observations.problem: Filesystem mtimes can drift after a distribution ZIP is created, making checked-in source freshness verification disagree with the packaged source snapshot.
observations.why_it_matters: Release closeout evidence should verify the package that will be reviewed, not incidental workspace mtimes after packaging.
observations.automation_candidate: Use --zip-metadata and --zip-timestamp-timezone on release-closeout-batch-manifest promote and verify when a release ZIP exists.
observations.suggested_followup: Pass RELEASE_CLOSEOUT_BATCH_MANIFEST_ZIP_METADATA during final packaging so source_evidence_freshness uses ZIP member timestamps.

observations.problem: Root-only tmp/*.json cleanup missed nested diagnostics and non-JSON temporary helper files.
observations.why_it_matters: A sealed release workspace can otherwise carry untracked scratch artifacts into operator review.
observations.automation_candidate: Make tmp cleanup and verify inspect the full tmp tree rather than only root JSON files.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep tmp-json-clean in closeout recipes before sealing checks; rename the target in a future compatibility window if the broader semantics need clearer UX.

observations.surface: routing provenance aggregate and auto-improve session rollups
observations.problem: Session-declared run IDs could point to run telemetry moved under runs/archive, leaving telemetry coverage at zero despite valid archived artifacts.
observations.why_it_matters: Learning readiness and loop-health diagnostics depend on discovering historical run telemetry consistently after archive compaction.
observations.automation_candidate: Resolve run artifacts through active runs and runs/archive candidates for telemetry, routing, executor, promotion, and fingerprint reports.
observations.suggested_followup: Use the archived telemetry regression test as the guard when changing run archive layout or session normalization.

observations.problem: A mechanism promotion could be supported by changed-files evidence that only touched tests, reports, or generated artifacts while leaving the primary mechanism target unchanged.
observations.why_it_matters: Promotion decisions should prove the scoped mechanism itself changed, not only its surrounding evidence.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a changed_files_manifest_primary_targets_touched blocker rule to the system_mechanism rule registry.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep generated/report-only fixes in maintenance lanes unless a scoped primary target mechanism change is present in the changed-files manifest.

observations.problem: Historical repeated same-eval runs had enough telemetry to audit, but lacked complete same-eval reason and behavior-delta digest coverage for a confirmed learning-improvement claim.
observations.why_it_matters: Release validation should not imply a learning improvement unless the repeated-run evidence has explicit reason, digest, telemetry, and placeholder-absence coverage.
observations.automation_candidate: Score repeated same-eval learning evidence with a schema-backed learning delta scoreboard before allowing improvement claims.
observations.suggested_followup: Use ops/reports/learning-delta-scoreboard.json to distinguish release evidence from learning-improvement claims; backfill or regenerate repeated same-eval runs only if an operator wants to make a confirmed learning claim.
